refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain a teleoperation link availability of ≥ 0.999 through redundant communication paths and automated failover mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The high safety criticality requires explicit mention of redundancy to meet the 0.999 availability constraint, moving beyond a simple performance target to a functional capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous ping and throughput monitoring across primary and secondary links during a 72-hour stress test in a high-interference environment.
sub_frs: Execute seamless failover between redundant paths
Buffer safety-critical commands during transient drops

candidate_dps: Multi-path WAN bonding with automated packet-level failover
selected_dp.selected_dp: Multi-path WAN bonding with automated packet-level failover

coupling_check.notes: The DP is functionally independent but shares the physical network medium with telemetry.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of at least two independent network providers at the worksite.
Risk: Failover latency might exceed the safety timeout threshold.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure the DP explicitly includes a 'heartbeat' mechanism to trigger failover.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Handles the end-to-end logic for remote control, including stream encoding, command decoding, and link management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and data link layer connection to external service providers.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reporting link status and health metrics to the site manager.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary logic for link management resides here.
Only hosts the hardware terminal; logic is external.
Depends on the link but does not manage the bonding logic.
Too broad; would lead to high diffusion across sub-systems.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link bonding within the Teleoperation module minimizes interface complexity for the Vehicle module.
containment_risks: If the bonding logic fails, the Safety Services module may not receive the 'Stop' command in time.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a dedicated 'Link Manager' sub-component within the Teleoperation module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The multi-path bonding DP directly addresses the 0.999 availability requirement through redundancy.

diffusion_risks: Network Link interface must be standardized across all modules to prevent fragmentation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module is the most logical owner for link-layer logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; link availability can be measured at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation to the Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Formalize the 'Network Link' interface specification to include QoS parameters.

decision.justification: The high-level FR and DP are validated and assigned to a module. Decomposition into sub-functions (monitoring, failover, buffering) is required to reach implementation-ready specifications.
next_level_sub_frs: Execute seamless failover between redundant paths
Buffer safety-critical commands during transient drops

assumptions: Dual-provider coverage is available at the site.
